growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate-buffered mineral salt medium (+ vitamin B12, H2 electron donor)

up.method: GC (headspace, chlorinated ethenes); rate = mol product formed / time; reported in Table 1

sec.method: GC; dechlorination rate PCE->ethene (Table 1); D. mccartyi BTF08 pure culture with H2 as electron donor

growth_records.cite: Stefan Kruse et al. Interspecies metabolite transfer and aggregate formation in a co-culture of Dehalococcoides and Sulfurospirillum dehalogenating tetrachloroethene to ethene. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00887-6. [PMC8163811]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: D. mccartyi BTF08, electron donor H2, With vitamin B12, Dechlorination rate 9 +/- 0.3 uM day-1. 'The dechlorination rate (PCE to ethene) of the Sm/BTF08 co-culture was 4.5-fold higher compared to the D. mccartyi strain BTF08 pure c
growth_records.notes: Volumetric dechlorination (reductive dehalogenation) rate, not per-gDW. PCE is consumed and stoichiometrically reduced to ethene, so 9 uM/day applies to both PCE consumed and ethene produced. Definition given verbatim in Methods: 'conversion of 400 uM PCE into 400 uM ethene over 10 days resulted in a dechlorination rate of 40 uM PCE/day'. H2 supplied at 50 kPa to avoid electron-donor limitation. No specific growth rate or biomass yield reported for this pure culture.

growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate-buffered mineral salt medium (+ vitamin B12, H2 electron donor)

sec.compound: VC (vinyl chloride, final dechlorination product for strain 195)

growth_records.cite: Stefan Kruse et al. Interspecies metabolite transfer and aggregate formation in a co-culture of Dehalococcoides and Sulfurospirillum dehalogenating tetrachloroethene to ethene. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00887-6. [PMC8163811]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: D. mccartyi 195, electron donor H2, With vitamin B12, Dechlorination rate 15 +/- 0.4 uM day-1. Strain 195 dechlorinates PCE to VC.
growth_records.notes: Volumetric dechlorination rate (uM/day), not per-gDW. Strain 195 produces vinyl chloride (VC), not ethene, as terminal product. PCE consumed = VC produced stoichiometrically at 15 uM/day. Pure culture with H2 as electron donor. No growth rate/yield reported for this pure culture.
